name: Kafr el-Sheikh Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the wheat and rice harvests of the Nile Delta, central to Kafr el-Sheikh's agricultural identity
the waters of Lake Burullus and the Mediterranean coast
the soil of the Delta and the Arabic name of the governorate

description: A yellow field charged in the centre with the multi-coloured emblem of Kafr el-Sheikh Governorate.
history: The flag of Kafr el-Sheikh Governorate displays its emblem on a yellow field reflecting the wheat and rice of the Nile Delta, adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s.

features.symbolism: the wheat and rice harvests of the Nile Delta and the lakes of Kafr el-Sheikh
the multi-coloured emblem of Kafr el-Sheikh, evoking the Mediterranean coast, Lake Burullus, agricultural fields and the Arabic name of the governorate
